commit.message:
Forms: fix 'Don't show this again' checkbox only dismissing on Got it click (#47943)

* Forms: fix "Don't show this again" checkbox dismissing modal on close

The FormsHelpModal used the same handler for both the "Got it" button
and the modal's onRequestClose (X button / Escape). This meant checking
the checkbox and then closing via X permanently dismissed the modal,
with no way for the user to change their mind.

Split the handlers so the dismiss API call only fires when the user
explicitly clicks "Got it".

fix: CRLF line endings breaking entrypoint.sh on Linux

Windows git checkout produces CRLF files. When copied into the Ubuntu
container the shebang becomes '#!/bin/bash\r' which Linux cannot find,
giving 'exec: no such file or directory'.

Fixes:
- .gitattributes: force LF for all *.sh, Dockerfile, yml, json, js, css, html
- Dockerfile: install dos2unix and convert all scripts immediately after COPY
  (belt-and-braces — works even if .gitattributes isn't applied on checkout)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: plan output blank — attach before start; fix log demultiplexing

Critical fixes:
1. plan_container: attach() BEFORE start() so no output is missed
   - Previously: start → attach → missed all initial entrypoint output
   - Now: createContainer → attach → register listeners → start → all bytes captured
   - Added stream error handler and devLog for every data chunk

2. start_container + subscribe_logs: demux Docker multiplexed stream
   - container.logs() always uses 8-byte Docker frame headers even with Tty:true
   - Was sending raw muxed bytes to xterm.js (garbage/invisible output)
   - Now: docker.modem.demuxStream() strips headers, stdout/stderr both forwarded

3. Refactored createAndStart into createContainer (no start) + createAndStart
   - plan_container uses createContainer + manual start
   - normal/resume/new modes still use createAndStart

commit.message:
Fix intermittent OSX CI failures in StartsWithNoMatch_StringComparison and EndsWithNoMatch_StringComparison (#125963)

Apple's `NSString compare:options:range:locale:` is non-deterministic
for certain Unicode inputs (C1 control chars `\u0080`–`\u009F`), causing
`StartsWithNoMatch_StringComparison` and
`EndsWithNoMatch_StringComparison` to fail intermittently on macOS. The
existing `[ActiveIssue]` only covered `IsAppleMobile` (iOS/tvOS),
leaving macOS unguarded.

## Changes

- **`StartsWithNoMatch_StringComparison`**: Add `[ActiveIssue(...,
IsOSX)]` referencing issue #108832; replace four separate `Assert.Equal`
calls with a `foreach` loop using `Assert.True(expected == actual,
message)` that captures `length`, `mismatchIndex`, `comp`, char values
at the mismatch index, and the `expected`/`actual` boolean values for
easier debugging when re-enabled.

- **`EndsWithNoMatch_StringComparison`**: Same fix — same root cause
(same Apple native API path), same `[ActiveIssue]` gap, same diagnostic
improvement applied consistently.

The diagnostic message format:
```
String.StartsWith: length=3, mismatchIndex=1, comp=CurrentCulture, chars=(2,3), expected=True, actual=False
Span.StartsWith:   length=3, mismatchIndex=1, comp=CurrentCulture, chars=(2,3), expected=True, actual=False
```

## Analysis: Root cause identifiable from code inspection

A dump or repro isn't strictly necessary here — this is a
wrong-return-value bug, not a crash, so a dump wouldn't add much. The
root cause is identifiable from the code.

### What the test does

The test
([`StartsWithNoMatch_StringComparison`](https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/blob/main/src/libraries/Common/tests/Tests/System/StringTests.cs#L7311))
creates strings from chars `\u0001`–`\u0096` (control characters + basic
Latin), introduces a single-char mismatch at each position, then asserts
that culture-aware `StartsWith` returns consistent results between
calls:

```csharp
Assert.Equal(
    firstSpan.ToString().StartsWith(secondSpan.ToString(), StringComparison.CurrentCulture),
    firstSpan.StartsWith(secondSpan, StringComparison.CurrentCulture));
```

The failure (`Expected: True, Actual: False`) means the same underlying
comparison returns different results on different invocations for the
same input.

### Where it goes wrong

On Apple mobile in hybrid globalization mode, culture-aware `StartsWith`
routes through
[`GlobalizationNative_StartsWithNative`](https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/blob/main/src/native/libs/System.Globalization.Native/pal_collation.m#L268)
in `pal_collation.m`:

    prefixString.precomposedStringWithCanonicalMapping);
NSString *sourceStrComposed = RemoveWeightlessCharacters(
    sourceString.precomposedStringWithCanonicalMapping);

EXP-1431-1440: Clinical decision support & correction analysis

10 experiments on mixed-magnitude intervention and clinical tools:
- Mixed basal@10%+CR@30%: still 0/11 grade transitions (sim limited) (1431)
- Correction bolus paradox: well-cal 37% vs poor-cal 61% effective (1432)
- Effective basal fraction differs from scheduled (AID doubles a's) (1433)
- Grade D→C requires CR@50% for patient a (not 30%) (1434)
- ISF concordance 0.86: corrections 14% less effective than meals (1435)
- AID aggressiveness ↔ TIR r=-0.514: bad settings force aggression (1436)
- Variability decomposition: 5 basal-dom, 4 meal-dom, 2 correction (1437)
- Weekly prediction: current score best predictor (r=0.644) (1438)
- CR is most reliable intervention (+2.4% TIR mean) (1439)
- Clinical dashboard: 2 medium urgency, 10/11 need action (1440)

Pipeline v8: CR@50% for grade D, overcorrection monitoring,
AID aggressiveness diagnostic, variability-based routing.

commit.message:
r600: comprehensive ABI contract update — 12 reviewer gaps resolved

Second hostile peer review sweep raised 12 architectural gaps across 3
passes. After rubber-duck validation (8 reviews total) and ISA cross-
referencing, all gaps are now addressed in the ABI contract.

New §10b (5 gaps from second sweep):
- Gap 5 (CRITICAL): CF stack exhaustion — 2-tier write guard strategy
  with mathematical predication fallback when stack depth >= 3
- Gap 6 (CRITICAL): Unified 3-tier KCACHE routing policy — static-
  provable → KCACHE, dynamic-robust → VFETCH, dynamic-non-robust →
  KCACHE + MIN clamp
- Gap 7: CF clause fragmentation — write batching, bounds hoisting,
  PRED_SET reuse optimizations documented
- Gap 8: Descriptor shift — unified ABI accounting rule for reserved
  internal slots with physical device limit deduction
- Gap 9: STORE_TYPED format conversion — probe-gated optimization only

New §12 Compiler Optimization Requirements:
- CF stack depth tracking (MANDATORY)
- 3-tier UBO routing (MANDATORY)
- Write guard batching, loop-invariant hoisting, PRED_SET reuse

New §11 open questions 7-9:
- STORE_TYPED UINT32 bit-transparency probe
- NULL-page write behavior for math predication
- CF stack depth mod-4 constraint

Blueprint updates:
- robust_read_access: 3-tier routing documented
- robust_write_access: 2-tier guard strategy documented
- Slot 0 aliasing RESOLVED: descriptor shift with MUTABLE_BASE 2→3
- TERAKAN_INTERNAL_RESERVED_MUTABLE_SLOTS constant added

New probe designs:
- Probe 5: STORE_TYPED UINT32 bit-transparency
- Probe 6: NULL-page MEM_RAT write safety

Appendix A updated: 25 findings from 8 reviews (was 18 from 6).

Make --target optional for full creation scripts; fix duplicate function drops

When --target is omitted, pg-deploy uses an empty schema as the target
so every source object becomes an ADD. The generated script header says
'PostgreSQL Full Creation Script' and indicates creation mode. This is
useful for bootstrapping a new empty database from DDL files.

functions by extracting parameter signatures from DDL and including them
in DROP statements. Identical drops are deduplicated via HashSet.

parameter signature extraction, overload deduplication, and full
creation mode.

guard that resolves DurableTaskClient from DI when the parameter carries

invocation path (FakeDurableTaskClientInputConverter).

commit.message:
fix: render parented GUI elements in world space with camera

GUI elements (like health bars) that are children of world-space
objects now render inside the camera transform. Previously all GUI
rendered in screen space, so a health bar parented to a player would
drift at double speed when the camera followed the player — the
parent's world-space movement was applied but the camera offset was
not, causing a space mismatch.

The RenderingEngine now checks if a GUI element is a Component with
a parent transform, and if so renders it in world space alongside
other world objects. Parentless GUI (HUD elements like FPSCounter)
continues to render in screen space.
